en: You can get file name, path, size and content
Loops over json entries/keys/indices
will return last http response
[skJson] No official English guide provided.
Formats the skript variable to json. This is used to format the skript variable to json.
[skJson] No official English guide provided.
[skJson] No official English guide provided.
[skJson] No official English guide provided.
Returns the value of the json at the given path. If the path is not provided, it will return all values from the json.
If the path is a number, it will return the value at that index in the array.
If the path is a string, it will return the value at that key in the object.
If the path is empty, it will return all values from the json.
Unbinds the storage for the given id. This is used to unbind the storage from the file.
Allows you create an simply string if for bound json file
Saves the cached json to a file. This is used to save the cache to a file.
Sends a previously created HTTP request to the specified URL
The request must be created using request creation commands (e.g. 'create request').
Supports both synchronous and asynchronous sending using the 'non' or 'not' tag.
Creates a virtual json storage. This is used to create a virtual json storage that can be used to store data in memory.
Maps the json to a skript list variable. This is used to map the json to a skript list variable.
Counts the number of keys or values in the json. This is used to count the number of keys or values in the json.
will only run when the json watcher notices a change in the file
Returns all json files from the given directory. The directory must be a valid path.
Get cached json storage. This is used to get the cached json storage. If the id is not provided, it will return all json storages.
That will allow loop through json, and get key/index or value
**json-value**, **json-key**
You can check if the inserted keys or values already in your specified json
You can get type of given Json.
Returns all keys from the given json object. If the path is provided, it will return all keys from the object at that path.
Represent response of http request
You can get those values (status, status code, body, headers)
Get last response from request
Returns the size of the json object or array.
If the json is null or primitive, it will return null.
If the json is an object, it will return the number of keys.
If the json is an array, it will return the number of elements.
value-expression for getting uuid from current watcher event
Returns the uuid of the watcher
value-expression for getting json content from current watcher event
Returns json element (content) of the watched|changed file
Json element representation
Representation instance of Response
Create new Attachment for the web request from path to file, when the file starts with */ the file will be found automatically.
Allows you print out the json with formatted format, 4 tab padding and colored for some elements
Returns the first, second, third, last, random or custom value of the json. This is used to get the first, second, third, last, random or custom value of the json.
set/add/reset or get the current request attachment
set/add/reset or get the current request query params
Receive response status
That could be OK, UNKNOWN or FAILED
Receive response status code
That could be anything from 100 to 504
Representation instance of Request
value-expression for getting file/link from current watcher event
Returns path of changed|watched file
Binds or unbinds a storage watcher to a file. This is used to watch for changes in the file and update the cache accordingly.
Creates a JSON file with the given name and content. If the file already exists, it will be replaced if the configuration is set to replace=true.
If the content is not provided, an empty JSON object will be created.
The configuration can be used to set the encoding and other options.
Creates a new json element from any object, file or website.
This is used to create a new json element from any object, file or website.
allowed methods are [GET, POST, PUT, HEAD, MOCK, DELETE, PATCH]
allowed value type of content is Json or stringify json (Json as String) e.g. "{""Test"": true}"
allowed value type of header is Json or (Pairs e.g. "Content-Type: application/Json", "Allow: restrict")
You can execute the request by 'send prepared {_request}', otherwise the request will be not sent, but the request will be still stored
And you can get response status/content/headers like in the examples
set/reset or get the current request body
get response body
set or get the current request headers
get response headers
set/get or set requests configuration for follow redirects
set/get or reset the current http version of request
set/get or reset the current timeout of request
Gets, sets, removes values/keys etc.. of JSON
Changer.
You can SET/ADD/REMOVE/RESET/DELETE/REMOVE_ALL
Sorts the JSON element in descending/ascending order by key/value.
The JSON element can be a JSON object or an array.
The sort type can be specified as a string in the format of 'by key ascending' or 'by value descending'.
The sort type can also be specified as a SortType enum value.
Explanatory notes
* - This expression is used to get a values (list) from a json object|array.
- Need to be at the end of the path.
This syntax is a simplification for json path, and shortening the notation and getting closer to the form (jq or other popular tools)
**RECOMMENDATION**: Use this syntax exclusively for paths that are a maximum of 2 keys deep. - for Creating
- Use rather value/key path expression
Can handle strict (get/set/remove)
all variables or expressions must be in “% ... %”
This syntax is strictly limited to its intended use; it does allow other expressions or variables to be used.
**RECOMMENDATION**: Use this syntax exclusively for paths that are a maximum of 2 keys deep.
Can handle strict (get/set/remove)
* at the end means you want to return a skript list.
